executive_summary: Total sales inc VAT for the week ending 16-May-2026 were 368,436.45, with sales ex VAT of 340,018.57 and an overall store margin of 17.53%.
Total waste for the week was 8,111.35, representing 2.39% of sales ex VAT, which is above the acceptable threshold and requires immediate management attention.

The department P&L total reports a combined margin of -70.22% on departmental sales of 62,099.87, driven primarily by severe anomalies in the Cigarettes department where a closing stock figure of 823.90 against an opening of 102,500.77 has produced a cost of sales of 101,676.87 and a reported loss of -100,392.45 at -7,816.16% margin - this is almost certainly a stock count or data entry error and must be investigated immediately.

Deli is reporting a loss of -2,226.96 at -149.61% margin due to a missing closing stock figure; the cost of sales has defaulted entirely to the opening stock value of 3,715.48, rendering the P&L for this department unreliable until a closing count is submitted.

Poultry is the strongest performing department by sales value at 25,390.16 and a margin of 98.93%, though the cost of sales of 271.12 against sales of that level warrants verification of opening and closing stock accuracy.

Fish waste of 862.65 and Provisions waste of 1,136.11 are the two highest non-Deli waste lines and together account for 24.6% of total store waste, signalling ordering and rotation issues in both departments.

Scan margins across fresh departments are running well above expected norms - Lamb at 101.91%, Bacon and Loose SRP at 117.90%, and Produce at 112.12% all indicate negative cost of sales figures, suggesting stock builds, unrecorded purchases, or count inaccuracies rather than genuine trading performance.
biggest_wins: Poultry: Sales of 25,390.16 at a reported margin of 98.93% make this the top revenue-generating department for the week; scan margin of 16.41% is more credible and suggests strong throughput with controlled shrink.
Fish: Sales of 6,083.82 at a reported margin of 99.44%, with a scan margin of 19.18% indicating solid product movement; waste of 862.65 remains a concern but underlying sales performance is strong.

Beef: Sales of 12,520.90 with a credible scan margin of 20.51% and a reported margin of 93.84%; cost of sales of 770.87 against strong sales suggests well-managed stock levels and minimal shrink outside of waste.
Provisions: Sales of 4,358.19 at a scan margin of 19.71% with very low cost of sales of 55.65, indicating tight stock management, though waste of 1,136.11 partially offsets this performance.
Produce: Sales of 6,547.11 with a scan margin of 19.13%; negative cost of sales of -793.52 inflates the reported margin to 112.12% and must be corrected, but underlying sales volume is positive.
biggest_risks: Cigarettes: Opening stock of 102,500.77 collapsing to a closing stock of 823.90 has generated a cost of sales of 101,676.87 and a reported loss of -100,392.45 at -7,816.16% margin. This is almost certainly a stock count error, a system import failure, or a misposted stock figure. This must be escalated to the store manager and accounts team before end of business Monday for investigation and correction.

Deli: Missing closing stock has caused the full opening stock value of 3,715.48 to flow through as cost of sales, resulting in a reported loss of -2,226.96 at -149.61% margin. A closing stock count must be completed and submitted immediately to produce a valid P&L. Deli waste of 1,253.18 further compounds profitability risk in this department.

Provisions Waste: Waste of 1,136.11 is the second highest in the store and represents a significant margin leak, particularly given the department's relatively modest sales base of 4,358.19. Ordering volumes and rotation disciplines must be reviewed urgently.

Deli Waste: Waste of 1,253.18 is the single highest waste line in the store. Combined with the absent closing stock and negative margin, Deli is the highest-risk department this week from both a data integrity and a shrink perspective.

Fish Waste: Waste of 862.65 against sales of 6,083.82 represents approximately 14.2% waste-to-sales in this department, well above acceptable levels. Ordering frequency, delivery quantities, and date rotation must be reviewed.

Negative Cost of Sales in Multiple Departments: Lamb (-30.33), Bacon and Loose SRP (-201.47), Produce (-793.52), and Poultry (271.12 against 25,390.16 in sales) all show cost of sales figures that are inconsistent with normal trading. These are indicative of stock count over-reports at closing or under-reports at opening and must be audited.

Overall Departmental P&L Reliability: The combined departmental figures showing a total loss of -43,606.51 at -70.22% margin are not a reflection of true trading performance but are distorted by the Cigarettes stock anomaly and missing Deli closing count. Until these are corrected, the departmental P&L cannot be used for management decision-making this week.

Bread and Cakes Waste: Waste of 735.63 is a material figure and, combined with dairy waste of 379.40, indicates that short-life ordering in ambient and chilled categories needs closer daily management.
department_callouts: Cigarettes: Closing stock of 823.90 against an opening of 102,500.77 is almost certainly erroneous. Cost of sales of 101,676.87 and a margin of -7,816.16% are not tradeable figures. Immediate investigation of the stock count and system data is required. Do not use this week's P&L for this department until corrected.
Beef: Scan margin of 20.51% and sales of 12,520.90 represent a solid week. Reported margin of 93.84% is inflated by stock movement; true performance should be benchmarked against the scan margin.

Lamb: Negative cost of sales of -30.33 has produced a reported margin of 101.91%. Closing stock of 1,305.81 is higher than opening of 1,275.48, indicating a stock build or count discrepancy. Verify count accuracy.
Pork: Scan margin of 20.65% is the strongest of the meat sub-departments on a like-for-like basis. Waste of 81.87 is well-controlled. Reported margin of 87.84% is credible directionally.

Bacon and Loose SRP: Negative cost of sales of -201.47 and reported margin of 117.90% are not credible trading figures. Closing stock of 1,044.86 exceeds opening of 843.39 with no recorded purchases, suggesting an uncaptured delivery or a count error. Scan margin of 11.62% is the most reliable indicator of true performance.

Poultry: The highest sales department this week at 25,390.16. Cost of sales of 271.12 against that revenue level implies either a very large closing stock build or missing purchase data. Validate stock count and confirm all purchase invoices are posted. Scan margin of 16.41% is the operative figure.

Fish: Strong sales of 6,083.82 but waste of 862.65 is critically high at approximately 14.2% of department sales. Daily waste recording, ordering review, and markdown strategy should be implemented immediately.

Produce: Sales of 6,547.11 with a scan margin of 19.13% are positive. Negative cost of sales of -793.52 inflates the reported margin to 112.12% and is likely a stock count over-report at closing. Waste of 1,393.82 is the highest in the fresh produce area and must be addressed through reduced order quantities and improved rotation.

Deli: Missing closing stock renders this week's P&L invalid. Waste of 1,253.18 is the highest single waste line in the store. Scan margin of 41.51% is unusually high and may reflect production cost inaccuracies or recipe discrepancies. Full review of Deli operations, waste recording, and stock counting procedures is required.

Provisions: Low cost of sales of 55.65 and a credible scan margin of 19.71% indicate sound trading. However, waste of 1,136.11 is disproportionately high relative to sales of 4,358.19 and represents approximately 26.1% waste-to-sales. Ordering must be reduced and daily rotation enforced.
Dairy: Waste of 379.40 is material. Review ordering levels, check rotation, and ensure date-checking is completed on every delivery.
Bread and Cakes: Waste of 735.63 signals over-ordering or insufficient markdown activity late in the trading day. A structured end-of-day reduction programme should be in place for this category.
Beer, Wine and Spirits: Waste of 42.25 in a typically low-waste category should be investigated for breakage recording accuracy and potential theft.
action_plan: ACTION 1 - IMMEDIATE (Monday AM): Investigate the Cigarettes department stock count. The closing figure of 823.90 against an opening of 102,500.77 is almost certainly incorrect. Pull the original count sheets, verify the system import, and repost the correct closing stock. Escalate to the store manager and area accountant before any further P&L reporting is produced.

ACTION 2 - IMMEDIATE (Monday AM): Complete and submit a closing stock count for Deli. The absence of a closing figure has rendered the department P&L unusable. Once submitted, review the corrected margin and scan margin discrepancy.

ACTION 3 - By Tuesday: Conduct a full stock count audit on Lamb, Bacon and Loose SRP, Produce, and Poultry where negative or implausibly low cost of sales figures have been produced. Identify whether the issue lies with opening counts, closing counts, or unposted purchase invoices, and correct accordingly.

ACTION 4 - By Tuesday: Review all outstanding purchase invoices across all departments to ensure every delivery received in the week has been posted to the system before the P&L is considered final. Particular attention to Poultry, where a cost of sales of 271.12 against sales of 25,390.16 is not credible.

ACTION 5 - By Wednesday: Deli and Provisions waste review. With combined waste of 2,389.29 between these two departments, the department managers must present a waste reduction plan to the store manager covering order quantity reductions, production scheduling for Deli, and daily rotation checklists for Provisions.

ACTION 6 - By Wednesday: Fish department waste action plan. Waste of 862.65 against sales of 6,083.82 is unsustainable. Review order quantities for the coming week, implement a daily markdown trigger for fish approaching use-by date, and ensure waste is being recorded at point of disposal rather than end of week.

ACTION 7 - By Wednesday: Produce waste review. Waste of 1,393.82 is the highest fresh waste line. Review the previous four weeks of orders versus sales to identify chronically over-ordered lines. Introduce a daily fresh check and reduce order quantities by a minimum of 10% on the top five wasted lines.

ACTION 8 - Ongoing this week: Implement a structured end-of-day markdown programme for Bread and Cakes. Waste of 735.63 indicates insufficient sell-through of short-life lines. Set a markdown trigger time of at least two hours before close and track markdown revenue versus waste value weekly.

ACTION 9 - Ongoing this week: Dairy waste of 379.40 - assign a responsible team member to complete a date rotation check on every dairy delivery and conduct a daily front-of-house date check. Adjust order quantities for the slowest-moving dairy lines.

ACTION 10 - By Friday: Review Deli scan margin of 41.51% against expected norms. Investigate whether recipe costings are accurate, whether all production inputs are being recorded, and whether the waste of 1,253.18 is being correctly categorised and entered into the system.

ACTION 11 - By Friday: Bacon and Loose SRP scan margin of 11.62% versus a reported margin of 117.90% is a significant divergence. Once the stock count issue is resolved, compare the corrected margin to the scan margin and identify the root cause of the gap. Check whether SRP pricing is being applied correctly at the till.

ACTION 12 - Standing instruction for next week: All department managers must submit opening and closing stock counts on time, ensure all purchase invoices are posted within 24 hours of delivery, and record waste daily at point of disposal. The data quality issues present in this week's report are preventing accurate management of the business and must not be repeated in the week ending 23-May-2026.
